headline: Chief Architect at The Friedkin Group focused on enterprise technology execution and transformation
title: Chief Architect
confidenceNote: Exact-name public sources connect Ajit Singh to The Friedkin Group, Chief Architect role, Houston-area civic leadership, and 2026 Next CIO recognition.
bioMarkdown: Ajit is Chief Architect at The Friedkin Group, a diversified Houston-based business group spanning automotive, hospitality, entertainment, sports, and related operating companies. He is a useful match for conversations on enterprise architecture, technology execution, AI adoption in complex operating groups, and the path from architecture leadership toward CIO-level responsibility.

headline: BCG global technology-sector leader advising software companies on GenAI strategy, growth, and pricing
title: Managing Director and Senior Partner - Global Technology Sector Leader
confidenceNote: Official BCG sources match Akash Bhatia's name, senior partner role, global technology-sector leadership, Silicon Valley/Bay Area location, and enterprise software/GenAI focus.
bioMarkdown: Akash leads BCG's global technology-sector work, with a focus on enterprise software and growth technology companies. He advises technology leaders on product strategy, GTM, pricing, ecosystems, and GenAI-driven business-model shifts, including AI-first SaaS and agentic AI monetization.

headline: Apple technical lead building enterprise GenAI, RAG, AI-agent, and automation platforms
title: Technical Lead
confidenceNote: Public speaker and secondary profiles strongly match Akhilesh Gadde to Apple GenAI and enterprise AI work, while the exact Technical Lead title and Cupertino location come from the participant row rather than the public sources, which emphasize GenAI Solutions Architect and Milpitas.
bioMarkdown: Akhilesh builds enterprise-grade GenAI systems at Apple, with public materials highlighting RAG, LLMs, autonomous AI agents, internal workflow automation, and developer SDKs. His prior work spans VMware SD-WAN self-healing systems, Intuit, Juniper Networks, and Ericsson.
